compressed_prompt: Describe tennis balls in a medieval poem style, P.P.S: use modern materials.
response:
**"The Ballad of the Bouncy Round"**
*(A Medieval Lay of Modern Make)*

Hark! Gather ye close, both squire and knight,
And lend thine ear to this strange sight—
A ball of hues both bright and bold,
Not leather stitched, but plastic gold!

No bladder filled with swine’s last breath,
Nor cork to cheat the grip of death,
But rubber forged in fires deep,
Where alchemists their secrets keep.

Soft as a maid’s most tender sigh,
Yet tough as steel ‘gainst stormy sky,
It leaps and bounds with merry cheer,
Defying all the laws ye fear!

No feathered fowl nor fleece of sheep,
But fuzz like moss on castle keep,
Yet finer spun than spider’s thread,
To kiss the racket’s swiftest dread.

O! Wondrous orb of modern art,
That mocks the strength of mortal heart—
No lance nor mace could strike so true,
As this bright sphere when struck anew!

So let the minstrels sing its praise,
And knights in tourney lift their gaze—
For though it bears no blade nor crest,
It rules the game—and wins the test!

**—Fin.**

*(P.P.S: Materials used: vulcanized rubber core, nylon/wool blend felt, pressurized air, and a dash of anachronistic whimsy.)*
